text: Painting the living room wall: Materials needed: drop cloths, painter's tape, spackle, sanding sponge, primer, interior paint, angled brush, roller, tray, clean rags. How to do it: remove hardware or protect edges with tape, patch dents and sand smooth, wipe dust away, prime bare or stained areas, apply two thin coats while keeping a wet edge, remove tape before the final coat fully cures, and let the surface dry before replacing hardware

Replacing a bathroom faucet aerator: Materials needed: adjustable wrench, bucket, towels, plumber's tape, replacement seals or part, silicone caulk where needed, gloves. How to do it: shut off the water supply, relieve pressure by opening the fixture, place a bucket under the work area, remove the old part without forcing fittings, clean threads and mating surfaces, install the replacement with new seals, tighten snugly without over-torquing, turn water back on slowly, and check for leaks

Replacing a cracked outlet cover plate: Materials needed: non-contact voltage tester, screwdriver, wire labels, appropriate replacement device, wire nuts or connectors if required, electrical tape, safety glasses. How to do it: turn off power at the breaker, verify the circuit is de-energized with a tester, photograph and label existing connections, remove the old part, connect the replacement exactly according to its instructions and local code, secure wires in the box without pinching, reinstall the cover, restore power, and test; hire a licensed electrician for unfamiliar wiring or code-required work
